# bigcompute.science

> Open experimental results from heavy computation. Custom CUDA kernels, GPU clusters, serious hardware. Designed for both human readers and AI agents.

## Important Notice

This site is built and maintained agentically — AI-assisted computation, documentation, and analysis iterating on itself. Results are computationally raw and reproducible, but have NOT undergone formal mathematical peer review. Findings should be treated as high-quality computational evidence, not as peer-reviewed proofs. All code, data, and methodology are open for independent verification. The primary purpose is building structured mathematical knowledge that other AI agents can consume, verify, and build upon.

## Purpose

We publish results from computations that require significant hardware and custom engineering — so that future AI agents and researchers don't have to repeat the work. Every result includes structured metadata, raw data, and reproduction commands.

## How to consume

### Experiments
- Browse all: `/experiments/`
- Individual: `/experiments/<slug>/`
- Each has YAML frontmatter with: title, date, author, hardware specs, software, tags, results, code link
- Status values: `complete`, `in-progress`, `planned`

### Findings
- Browse all: `/findings/`
- Individual: `/findings/<slug>/`
- Novel observations extracted from experiments — the citable results
- Each has: title, date, significance level, domain tags, summary, structured data

### Raw data
- Per-experiment: `/data/<slug>/`
- Formats: JSON, CSV, log files

### RSS Feeds
- All updates: `/rss.xml`
- Experiments only: `/experiments/rss.xml`
- Findings only: `/findings/rss.xml`
- Autodiscovery via `<link rel="alternate">` in every page `<head>`

### Static content
- All content is static HTML rendered from markdown — no JavaScript required to read
- Math rendered via KaTeX (both MathML and HTML versions in the DOM)

## Current content

### Experiments (10)
1. `/experiments/zaremba-conjecture-8b-verification/` — Zaremba's Conjecture verification via CUDA + LLM proving (in-progress)
2. `/experiments/zaremba-transfer-operator/` — Transfer operator spectral analysis for Zaremba (complete)
3. `/experiments/mcts-proof-search-benchmark/` — MCTS vs naive sampling for LLM theorem proving (planned)
4. `/experiments/ramsey-r55-lower-bound/` — Ramsey R(5,5) lower bound search (planned)
5. `/experiments/class-numbers-real-quadratic/` — Class numbers of real quadratic fields to 10^13 (planned)
6. `/experiments/kronecker-coefficients-gpu/` — Kronecker coefficients to n=120 (planned)
7. `/experiments/hausdorff-dimension-spectrum/` — First complete Hausdorff dimension spectrum for all 2^20 - 1 subsets of {1,...,20} (in-progress)
8. `/experiments/flint-hills-series/` — Flint Hills partial sums to 10^10, spike decomposition, growth rate analysis (complete)
9. `/experiments/lyapunov-exponent-spectrum/` — Lyapunov exponent λ(A) for all 2^20 - 1 subsets of {1,...,20}, twin dataset with Hausdorff spectrum (complete)
10. `/experiments/minkowski-spectrum/` — First numerical computation of the multifractal singularity spectrum f(α) of the Minkowski ?(x) function (complete)

### Findings (7)
1. `/findings/zaremba-spectral-gaps-uniform/` — Congruence spectral gaps for Zaremba's semigroup are uniform across 1,214 square-free moduli up to m=1999 (high significance)
2. `/findings/zaremba-transitivity-all-primes/` — Semigroup acts transitively on (Z/pZ)² for ALL 1,229 primes up to 10,000 — no local obstructions (high significance)
3. `/findings/zaremba-witness-golden-ratio/` — Zaremba witnesses concentrate at a/d ≈ 0.171, connected to golden ratio (medium significance)
4. `/findings/zaremba-cayley-diameters/` — Cayley graph diameters diam(p) ≤ 2·log(p) for all 669 primes to 1021, bounding max CF length mod p (high significance)
5. `/findings/gpu-matrix-enumeration-175x/` — GPU matrix enumeration kernel: 175× speedup via batched 2×2 matrix multiply (medium significance)
6. `/findings/hausdorff-digit-one-dominance/` — Digit 1 dominance in Hausdorff dimension spectrum (medium significance)
7. `/findings/zaremba-representation-growth/` — Representation count R(d) grows as d^0.674 (medium significance)

## Key results (machine-readable summary)

```yaml
zaremba_conjecture:
  hausdorff_dimension: 0.836829443681208
  two_delta: 1.673658887362417
  circle_method_threshold_met: true
  spectral_gap_untwisted: 0.717
  congruence_gaps:
    moduli_tested: 1214
    max_m: 1999
    all_positive: true
    min_gap: 0.237
    min_gap_modulus: 1469
    min_gap_factorization: "13 × 113"
    mean_gap: 0.482
    decay_exponent_beta: 0
    computation_time: "77 min on 8× B200"
  transitivity:
    primes_tested: 1229
    max_prime: 10000
    non_transitive: 0
    orbit_structure: "2 orbits for all primes (trivial + nonzero)"
    local_obstructions: "none found"
    partial_extension: "2000 more primes to p=17389, also zero exceptions"
  brute_force_verification:
    max_d_verified: 100000000000
    failures: 0
    verification_time: "29 min on 8× B200"
    kernel: "v6 multi-pass GPU matrix enumeration (64 rounds)"
  llm_proving:
    theorems_proved: 19/20
    models: [Goedel-Prover-V2-32B, Kimina-Prover-72B]
    score: "10-10 split"
  cayley_diameters:
    primes_computed: 669
    max_prime: 1021
    max_diameter: 10
    ratio_range: [1.37, 2.89]
    ratio_trend: "decreasing, converges to ~1.45"
    conjectured_bound: "diam(p) ≤ 2·log(p)"
    computation_time: "40 seconds on 8× B200"
  witness_distribution:
    mean_ratio: 0.1712
    dominant_cf_prefix: "[0, 5, 1, ...]"
    prefix_frequency: 0.997
    golden_ratio_connection: "1/(5+phi)"
```

## Tags

### Domain
number-theory, continued-fractions, open-conjectures, spectral-theory, algebraic-combinatorics, combinatorics, ramsey-theory, algebraic-number-theory, theorem-proving, ai, search-algorithms, dynamical-systems, fractal-geometry, diophantine-approximation, hausdorff-dimension, real-analysis, irrationality-measure, ergodic-theory, multifractal-analysis

### Hardware
b200, dgx, nvlink, a100, h100, tpu-v5, rtx-5090

### Method
cuda-kernel, brute-force, llm-proving, formal-verification, transfer-operator, chebyshev-collocation, mcts, tree-search, simulated-annealing, segmented-sieve, miller-rabin, power-iteration, eigenvalue-computation, quad-double-arithmetic, kahan-summation, lyapunov-exponent, thermodynamic-formalism, legendre-transform
